topic: r6g Family Instance Types and Graviton2 Processor Performance

  entities:
  
    AWS|Company
    Graviton|Product
    Graviton2|Product
    r6g family|Product
    r6g.large|Product
    r6g.16xlarge|Product
    Intel|Company
    AMD|Company

  proposition: AWS has developed its own ARM-based processor called Graviton.
    
    entity-entity relationships:
    AWS|DEVELOPED|Graviton
    
    entity-attributes:
    Graviton|ARCHITECTURE|ARM-based
      
  proposition: Graviton is an ARM-based processor.
  
    entity-entity relationships:
  
    entity-attributes:
    Graviton|ARCHITECTURE|ARM-based
    Graviton|TYPE|processor

  proposition: AWS developed Graviton.

    entity-entity relationships:
    AWS|DEVELOPED|Graviton

    entity-attributes:

  proposition: Graviton delivers better price/performance than the Intel and AMD equivalents.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton|PERFORMANCE|better price/performance than Intel and AMD equivalents

  proposition: The r6g family uses the Graviton2 processor.

    entity-entity relationships:
    r6g family|USES|Graviton2

    entity-attributes:

  proposition: r6g is a family of instance types.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|TYPE|family of instance types

  proposition: Graviton2 is a processor.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|TYPE|processor

  proposition: The Graviton2 processor offers 10-20% better performance for OLTP-style (constrained) graph queries.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|PERFORMANCE|10-20% better for OLTP-style queries

  proposition: OLTP-style queries are constrained graph queries.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:

  proposition: Larger, OLAP-ish queries may be slightly less performant with the Graviton2 processors than with Intel ones.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|PERFORMANCE|slightly less performant for larger OLAP-ish queries than Intel

  proposition: The slightly less performant performance of larger, OLAP-ish queries with Graviton2 processors is owing to slightly less performant memory-paging performance.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    Graviton2|LIMITATION|slightly less performant memory-paging performance

  proposition: The r6g family has a single-socket architecture.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|ARCHITECTURE|single-socket

  proposition: The single-socket architecture means that performance scales linearly with compute capacity from an r6g.large to an r6g.16xlarge.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    r6g family|SCALING|linear performance scaling with compute capacity

  proposition: r6g.large is an instance type in the r6g family.

    entity-entity relationships:
    r6g.large|PART_OF|r6g family

    entity-attributes:
    r6g.large|TYPE|instance type

  proposition: r6g.16xlarge is an instance type in the r6g family.

    entity-entity relationships:
    r6g.16xlarge|PART_OF|r6g family

    entity-attributes:
    r6g.16xlarge|TYPE|instance type

  proposition: r6g.16xlarge is the largest type in the r6g family.

    entity-entity relationships:

    entity-attributes:
    r6g.16xlarge|SIZE|largest type in r6g family
